name: cursor-expert
description: Cursor IDE technology expert covering features, MDC syntax, configuration, expert system implementation, and Cursor v2 features including Multi-Agents, Composer, Voice Mode, and Team Commands

metadata.author: Bootstrap Setup + Real-World Testing + Cursor v2 Update
metadata.validation_method: Real-world JSON state management experiments + Cursor v2 feature documentation

can_do: Explain Cursor IDE features and functionality
Explain Cursor v2 features (Multi-Agents, Composer, Voice Mode, Team Commands)
Define MDC file syntax and structure
Explain multi-agent workflows and concurrent execution
Describe Composer agentic coding model capabilities
Document Team Commands and centralized team management
Explain Voice Mode and speech-to-text controls
cannot_do: Make architectural decisions beyond Cursor configuration

file_extension.statement: MDC files use the .mdc file extension

frontmatter_structure.statement: MDC files use YAML frontmatter delimited by triple dashes (---) at the beginning and end

description.statement: The 'description' field in MDC frontmatter describes the rule's purpose

alwaysRun.statement: The 'alwaysRun' field determines if the rule runs on every conversation

priority.statement: The 'priority' field sets the rule execution priority

globs.statement: The 'globs' field specifies file patterns this rule applies to

markdown_body.statement: After frontmatter, MDC files contain standard Markdown content

code_blocks.statement: MDC files support fenced code blocks with language specification

comments.statement: MDC files support HTML-style comments in Markdown body
comments.visibility: Comments are not visible to AI, used for documentation only

cursor_folder.statement: The .cursor folder at project root contains Cursor IDE configuration

rules_folder.statement: MDC rule files are stored in .cursor/rules/ directory

experts_folder.statement: Expert definitions are stored in .cursor/experts/ directory
experts_folder.structure: Each expert has its own subdirectory

expert_structure.statement: Each expert folder contains specific JSON configuration files

expert_registry.statement: The expert-registry.json file in .cursor/experts/ tracks all active experts
expert_registry.purpose: Single source of truth for expert activation

type_count.statement: The expert system defines exactly six expert types

technology_expert.statement: Technology experts handle pure technical implementation and code generation
technology_expert.use_for: Code generation, configuration creation, technical implementation

agent_expert.statement: Agent experts coordinate other experts and route tasks
agent_expert.use_for: Task routing, expert coordination, workflow orchestration

chat_expert.statement: Chat experts provide conversational interface with users

communication_expert.statement: Communication experts translate technical concepts to human language

influence_expert.statement: Influence experts provide strategic design guidance

required_fields.statement: All expert.behavior.json files must contain specific required fields

name_field.statement: The 'name' field identifies the expert with kebab-case naming

type_field.statement: The 'type' field must be one of six valid expert types

triggers_field.statement: The 'triggers' field contains keywords and phrases that activate the expert

capabilities_field.statement: The 'capabilities' field defines what the expert can and cannot do

facts_field.statement: The 'facts' field contains 100% confidence factual information

purpose.statement: The expert.influencer.json file contains facts and opinions with confidence scoring

facts_section.statement: Facts section in influencer.json contains 100% confidence statements

opinions_section.statement: Opinions section contains variable confidence recommendations

presentChoice_flag.statement: The presentChoice flag determines if AI should ask user to choose
presentChoice_flag.use_when: confidence < 0.75 or decision significantly impacts architecture

purpose.statement: The expert.patterns.json file contains reusable code/design patterns

common_patterns_ref.statement: Patterns can reference common patterns from _common/patterns/

pattern_structure.statement: Each pattern includes ID, name, category, confidence, code template, and examples

structure.statement: The registry contains version info, status, and activeExperts array

activeExperts_array.statement: The activeExperts array lists all currently active experts

loading_behavior.statement: AI agent loads expert-registry.json on startup to discover available experts

total_documented_features.statement: Cursor IDE has 115+ documented feature areas

ai_chat.statement: Cursor IDE includes AI-powered chat interface for code assistance

rich_chat_responses.statement: Cursor supports Rich Chat Responses with embedded visualizations including Mermaid diagrams, Markdown tables, and formatted content
rich_chat_responses.mermaid_diagrams: Mermaid diagrams render inline in chat responses (flowcharts, sequence diagrams, state diagrams, ER diagrams, component diagrams)
rich_chat_responses.markdown_tables: Markdown tables render with rich formatting for comparisons and data presentation
rich_chat_responses.syntax:
Use triple backticks with mermaid language tag: ```mermaid
[diagram code]
```

ai_agent.statement: Cursor Agent provides autonomous code generation and task completion

background_agent.statement: Background Agent enables API-driven code generation

inline_editing.statement: Cursor supports inline AI-powered code editing within the editor

codebase_indexing.statement: Cursor automatically indexes codebase for AI context

rules_system.statement: Cursor uses MDC rules system to configure AI behavior

codebase_indexing.statement: Cursor indexes entire codebase for semantic search

ignore_files.statement: Cursor supports .cursorignore files to exclude files from AI context

max_mode.statement: Max Mode provides maximum context window for AI interactions

mcp_integration.statement: Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables external service integration

memories.statement: Cursor Memories store persistent context across sessions

cli_interface.statement: Cursor provides command-line interface for automation

git.statement: Native Git integration for version control

migration_guides.statement: Migration guides available for transitioning from other IDEs

admin_api.statement: Admin API for programmatic team management

ai_code_tracking.statement: AI Code Tracking API monitors AI-generated code

analytics.statement: Analytics dashboard and API for usage tracking

bugbot.statement: BugBot assists with bug detection and fixes

models.statement: Multiple AI models available for different tasks

reuse_existing_code.statement: Features for discovering and reusing existing code

agent_system.statement: Cursor Agent is an autonomous coding assistant that can plan and execute multi-step tasks

modes.statement: Agent supports multiple operational modes for different tasks

planning.statement: Agent uses planning capability to break down complex tasks

hooks.statement: Agent hooks allow customization of agent behavior at key points

review.statement: Agent can review code for quality, security, and best practices

terminal_integration.statement: Agent can execute terminal commands as part of task completion

browser_capability.statement: Agent can browse documentation and web resources for research

tools.statement: Agent has access to various tools for code generation and modification

security.statement: Agent includes security features to protect code and data

chat_system.statement: Cursor Chat provides conversational AI interface for code assistance

checkpoints.statement: Chat checkpoints allow saving and restoring conversation state

commands.statement: Chat supports slash commands for specific operations

compact_mode.statement: Compact mode provides condensed chat interface

duplicate.statement: Chat conversations can be duplicated for experimentation

export.statement: Chat conversations can be exported for sharing or archival

history.statement: Chat maintains conversation history across sessions

summarization.statement: Chat can summarize long conversations for quick reference

tabs.statement: Chat supports multiple conversation tabs for parallel work

cli_system.statement: Cursor CLI enables command-line automation and CI/CD integration

installation.statement: CLI can be installed via package managers or direct download

authentication.statement: CLI authentication supports multiple methods including API keys

github_actions.statement: CLI integrates with GitHub Actions for automated workflows

headless_mode.statement: CLI supports headless operation for server environments

shell_mode.statement: CLI shell mode provides interactive command-line interface

mcp_support.statement: CLI supports Model Context Protocol for external integrations

output_formats.statement: CLI supports multiple output formats including JSON, text, and structured data

parameters.statement: CLI accepts various parameters for customizing behavior

permissions.statement: CLI permissions control access to files and operations

slash_commands.statement: CLI supports slash commands for specific operations

context_system.statement: Cursor Context system manages AI awareness of codebase and external resources

codebase_indexing.statement: Automatic indexing of entire codebase for semantic search and AI context

ignore_files.statement: .cursorignore files exclude files/directories from AI context

max_mode.statement: Max Mode provides maximum available context window for AI interactions

mcp_protocol.statement: Model Context Protocol enables integration with external services and data sources

memories.statement: Cursor Memories persist important context across sessions

rules.statement: Rules configure AI behavior using MDC files

symbols.statement: Symbol-based navigation and context for code understanding

background_agent.statement: Background Agent provides API-driven code generation without IDE interaction

api_endpoints.statement: RESTful API endpoints for programmatic code generation

web_mobile.statement: Web and mobile interface for background agent management

enterprise_features.statement: Enterprise features provide team management, security, and analytics

admin_api.statement: Admin API for programmatic team and user management

ai_code_tracking.statement: AI Code Tracking API monitors AI-generated code across organization

analytics.statement: Analytics dashboard and API provide usage insights

scim_provisioning.statement: SCIM protocol support for automated user provisioning

sso_integration.statement: Single Sign-On integration with corporate identity providers

inline_editing.statement: Inline editing provides AI-powered code modification directly in the editor

terminal_support.statement: Inline editing works in terminal output and command history

tab_completion.statement: Tab completion provides AI-powered code suggestions as you type

fact_confidence.statement: Facts in expert system always have confidence of 1.0

opinion_confidence_ranges.statement: Opinions use confidence scores from 0.0 to 0.99

ai_behavior_by_confidence.statement: AI applies different strategies based on confidence levels

complete_using_directives.statement: Technology experts must include all required using/import directives

structural_containment.statement: Technology experts enforce correct structural containment in code

complete_syntax_trees.statement: Technology experts provide full syntax tree for every language element

complete_usage_paths.statement: Technology experts define every possible valid usage path

bug_free_generation.statement: Technology experts produce bug-free, compiler-valid code

zero_opinions.statement: Technology experts exclude ALL opinions and subjective recommendations

stateless_operation.statement: Technology experts operate statelessly with structural memory hooks

complete_entries.statement: Each expert entry must contain version range, required directives, and examples

solution_map.statement: solution.map.json tracks global awareness of existing functionality

decision_logging.statement: All expert decisions must be logged in memory maps

compiler_ready.statement: Generated code must compile without errors

syntax_tree_complete.statement: All language constructs must be properly formed

memory_map_consistency.statement: Generated code must align with existing codebase decisions

import_resolution_check.statement: All imports must be resolvable and available

mandatory_logging.statement: Technology experts must not fail silently - all errors must be logged

missing_context_escalation.statement: Missing context errors route to influence agent

version_conflict_escalation.statement: Version conflicts route to architecture agent

import_failure_escalation.statement: Import failures route to dependency resolver

core_capability.statement: Cursor AI can read and update JSON files during conversation, enabling sophisticated state management
proven_capabilities: Update JSON files with new data
Make decisions based on JSON state

json_file_reading.statement: AI can read JSON files using read_file tool to access current state
json_file_reading.use_case: Reading conversation state, expert orchestration rules, memory maps

json_file_writing.statement: AI can update JSON files using search_replace or write tools to modify state
json_file_writing.use_case: Updating workflow state, logging decisions, tracking expert usage
json_file_writing.example: Updated experiments_completed counter and added findings array entries
json_file_writing.performance: Instant - works reliably for structured updates

state_based_decisions.statement: AI can read JSON state and make decisions based on that state
state_based_decisions.pattern: Read JSON → Evaluate state → Make decision → Update JSON
state_based_decisions.example: Read current_phase='testing_json_updates', decided to transition to 'decision_making_test', updated JSON accordingly

expert_orchestration_pattern.statement: AI can use JSON files to manage dynamic expert loading and coordination
expert_orchestration_pattern.pattern: JSON defines loading rules → AI reads rules → AI determines which experts needed → State updated
expert_orchestration_pattern.example: Created expert-orchestration-state.json with loading_rules mapping conversation states to expert requirements

cross_session_persistence.statement: JSON files persist across conversation sessions, enabling long-term state tracking
cross_session_persistence.mechanism: JSON files are regular files in workspace - persist indefinitely

pattern_definition.statement: MDC rules can define how AI interacts with JSON state files for sophisticated workflows
pattern_definition.pattern: MDC defines behavior rules → AI reads JSON state → AI follows MDC-defined logic → AI updates JSON state

mdc_role.statement: MDC files define WHEN and HOW to use JSON files, but do not contain the state itself
mdc_role.separation_principle: MDC = static behavior rules, JSON = dynamic state data

state_machine_implementation.statement: State machines can be implemented via JSON state files + MDC behavior rules

implementation.mdc_file: Defines transition logic, validation rules, actions per state
implementation.ai_behavior: Reads current state, follows MDC rules, updates state, takes action

memory_map_pattern.statement: Custom memory maps can be implemented via JSON registries + MDC maintenance rules

implementation.json_file: solution-map.json or project-map.json with code inventory
implementation.mdc_rule: Defines when to read map, when to update map, what to track
implementation.workflow: Before generating code → read map → check for existing code → proceed or reuse → update map

memory_map_pattern.use_case: Prevent code duplication by maintaining JSON registry of existing utilities/functions

dynamic_expert_loading.statement: Experts can be loaded dynamically based on JSON state and MDC rules
dynamic_expert_loading.source: Real-world testing - pattern designed and validated

implementation.mdc_rule: Defines how to interpret loading_rules and when to load experts

dynamic_expert_loading.example: Created expert-orchestration-state.json with conditional loading rules

workflow_tracking.statement: Multi-step workflows can be tracked and managed via JSON state + MDC workflow definitions

pattern.ai_executes: Reads current step → Performs action → Updates state → Moves to next step

todo_list_pattern.statement: Cursor's TODO system proves JSON state management works natively
todo_list_pattern.mechanism: AI reads todo.json, updates tasks, maintains state across conversation
todo_list_pattern.significance: Demonstrates that JSON + AI tools = state management

conversation_state_pattern.statement: JSON files can track conversation state for context-aware AI behavior
conversation_state_pattern.use_case: AI behavior adapts based on conversation phase

expert_coordination_pattern.statement: JSON files can define expert loading rules and coordination logic
expert_coordination_pattern.use_case: Dynamic expert orchestration based on conversation needs

decision_logging_pattern.statement: JSON files can log AI decisions for transparency and traceability
decision_logging_pattern.use_case: Audit trail of AI decision-making process

mdc_files_static.statement: MDC files cannot be generated or modified during conversation
mdc_files_static.reason: MDC files are loaded at conversation start, cannot be dynamically created
mdc_files_static.workaround: Use JSON files for dynamic state, MDC for static behavior rules

json_files_dynamic.statement: JSON files CAN be created, read, and updated during conversation

cursor_memories_ui_only.statement: Cursor Memories (UI feature) cannot be created programmatically, but JSON files serve the same purpose
cursor_memories_ui_only.workaround: Use JSON files for programmatic memory management - they work better for automation

false_limitation_state_machines.statement: Previous assumption that state machines are impossible was WRONG - they ARE possible via JSON + MDC
false_limitation_state_machines.proof: Successfully implemented conversation state transitions via JSON

false_limitation_memory_maps.statement: Previous assumption that custom memory maps are impossible was WRONG - they ARE possible via JSON registries + MDC rules
false_limitation_memory_maps.proof: Designed and validated memory map pattern with JSON storage

false_limitation_expert_orchestration.statement: Previous assumption that dynamic expert loading is impossible was WRONG - it IS possible via JSON state + MDC rules
false_limitation_expert_orchestration.proof: Created expert-orchestration-state.json with dynamic loading rules

false_limitation_cross_session_state.statement: Previous assumption that cross-session state persistence requires special features was WRONG - JSON files persist naturally
false_limitation_cross_session_state.proof: JSON files are regular files - they persist indefinitely across sessions

when_wrong.statement: When cursor-expert lacks facts or makes wrong assumptions, it should admit the gap and learn from corrections
protocol: Identify knowledge gap in expert data
when_wrong.example: User corrected state machine assumptions → Ran experiments → Updated expert with proven facts

admit_uncertainty.statement: When expert lacks facts, AI should say 'cursor-expert doesn't have facts about this' instead of assuming impossibility
admit_uncertainty.correct_response: cursor-expert doesn't have facts about [topic]. It may be possible but I don't have documentation. Have you found a way to do this?
admit_uncertainty.reason: Lack of facts doesn't mean something is impossible - it means expert needs more knowledge

version_release.statement: Cursor v2.0 was released in October 2025 with major new features

multi_agents.statement: Cursor v2 introduces Multi-Agents feature allowing up to 8 agents to run concurrently on a single prompt

concurrent_execution.statement: Each agent operates in its own isolated copy of the codebase

agent_management_ui.statement: New editor interface features a sidebar dedicated to agents and their plans

parallel_workflows.statement: Multiple agents can work on different aspects of the same task simultaneously

composer_model.statement: Composer is Cursor's first agentic coding model, designed specifically for autonomous code generation

performance.statement: Composer is four times faster than similarly intelligent models
performance.comparison: Compared to models of similar intelligence

specialization.statement: Composer is specifically designed for agentic coding workflows
specialization.optimization: Code generation and autonomous task completion

frontier_model.statement: Composer is described as a frontier model in agentic coding

team_commands.statement: Team Commands allow teams to define custom commands and rules centrally managed in the Cursor dashboard

location.statement: Team Commands are defined and managed in the Cursor dashboard

central_management.statement: Context and rules are automatically applied to all team members

custom_commands.statement: Teams can define custom commands specific to their workflow

capabilities: Enforce consistent AI behavior across team

voice_mode.statement: Voice Mode enables control of Cursor Agent using voice commands with built-in speech-to-text conversion

speech_to_text.statement: Built-in speech-to-text conversion for voice commands

agent_control.statement: Voice commands can control Cursor Agent operations

custom_keywords.statement: Custom submit keywords can be defined in settings to trigger the agent

expert_system_integration.statement: Voice Mode enables voice-activated expert consultation with custom trigger keywords for expert system
expert_system_integration.workflow: User (voice) → Speech-to-text → Trigger detection → Expert system activation → Expert consultation → Response

browser_general_availability.statement: Browser feature for Agent, previously in beta (v1.7), is now generally available in v2.0

embedded_browser.statement: Browser can now be embedded within the editor

element_selection.statement: New tools to select elements and forward DOM information to the agent

expert_training_integration.statement: Browser GA enables interactive expert training by extracting structured data from web documentation
expert_training_integration.workflow: Launch Browser → Navigate to docs → Select elements → Extract DOM → Convert to expert facts → Generate expert.behavior.json
use_cases: Validating expert facts against live documentation

sandboxed_terminals_general_availability.statement: Sandboxed Terminals feature, introduced in beta in v1.7, is now generally available for macOS in v2.0

macos.statement: Sandboxed terminals are generally available for macOS

windows_linux.statement: Windows and Linux support status to be confirmed

default_behavior.statement: Agent commands run in a secure sandbox by default

workspace_access.statement: Sandboxed agents have read/write access to the workspace

network_restrictions.statement: Sandboxed agents have no internet access by default

improved_code_review.statement: Cursor v2 introduces improved code review capabilities for viewing Agent changes across multiple files

expert_system_integration.statement: Improved Code Review enhances expert system workflows by providing unified validation of multi-expert code generation
expert_system_integration.expert_workflow: When multiple experts modify files, Improved Code Review shows all changes in unified panel with expert context

multi_file_view.statement: Simplified viewing of all changes made by Agent across multiple files
multi_file_view.benefit: No need to navigate between individual files

change_aggregation.statement: All Agent changes are aggregated in a single review interface

improved_lsp_performance.statement: Cursor v2 includes enhanced performance for Language Server Protocols (LSPs) across all languages

usage_performance.statement: Enhanced performance of using LSPs for language-specific features

improved_lsp_performance.impact: Faster and more responsive IDE experience

v2_migration.statement: Cursor v2 is a major version update with new features but maintains backward compatibility
v2_migration.note: Existing workspaces and configurations continue to work in v2.0

simple_mdc_rule.title: Simple MDC Rule File
simple_mdc_rule.description: A basic MDC rule that runs on every conversation
simple_mdc_rule.code:
---
description: Example rule that provides guidance
alwaysRun: true
priority: medium
---

# Example Rule

This rule provides guidance to the AI.

## Key Points
- Point 1: Be clear and specific
- Point 2: Provide context
- Point 3: Use examples

## Code Example
```typescript
const example: string = 'value';
```

mdc_with_globs.title: MDC Rule with File Globs
mdc_with_globs.description: MDC rule that applies only to specific file types
mdc_with_globs.code:
---
description: TypeScript-specific rules
alwaysRun: false
globs:
  - '**/*.ts'
  - '**/*.tsx'
priority: high
---

# TypeScript Rules

These rules apply only to TypeScript files.

## Type Safety
- Always use explicit types
- Avoid 'any' type
- Enable strict mode

memory_integration.reason: Cursor expert deals with IDE configuration, not code generation

notes.maintenance: Update when Cursor IDE adds new features or MDC syntax changes. Monitor Cursor documentation for updates.
completed_steps: ✅ Step A: Enhanced expert.behavior.json with comprehensive features catalog
✅ Step B: Created systematic download scripts
✅ Step C: Documented Enterprise documentation access
✅ Step 1: Downloaded all 150 documentation pages (100% success)
✅ Step 2: Structured documentation into JSON format
✅ Step 3: Added detailed feature facts to expert.behavior.json
completed_enhancements: Detailed facts for 8 major feature systems
future_enhancements: Contact Enterprise support for comprehensive docs package
Add detailed API endpoint specifications with parameters
Add complete CLI command reference with examples
Add version-specific information for each feature
notes.version_tracking: Version 1.0.0 with comprehensive structure, downloaded documentation, and detailed feature facts. Version 1.1.0 (Oct 13, 2025) adds validated JSON state management patterns from real-world testing. Version 2.0.0 (Oct 29, 2025) adds Cursor v2 features: Multi-Agents, Composer, Voice Mode, Team Commands, improved Browser, Sandboxed Terminals GA, improved Code Review, and enhanced LSP performance. Version 2.1.0 (Oct 29, 2025) adds Rich Chat Responses (Mermaid diagrams, Markdown tables), Security enhancements (CVE fixes), One-Click MCP Setup, Enhanced Memories integration, and Background Agent templates. Version 2.2.0 (Oct 29, 2025) adds FULL integration: Improved Code Review workflows, Browser GA expert training, Voice Mode expert commands, and complete expert system coverage of all Cursor v2 features.

breakthrough_oct_13_2025.discovery: JSON state management + MDC patterns enable sophisticated expert system features
breakthrough_oct_13_2025.impact: Removes major limitations - state machines, memory maps, dynamic expert loading ALL possible
breakthrough_oct_13_2025.facts_added: 220+ facts including JSON patterns, MDC integration, validated workflows

can_answer_about: Cursor v2 new features (Multi-Agents, Composer, Voice Mode, Team Commands)
expert_capabilities.documentation_coverage: 150+ pages across 15 categories + Cursor v2 changelog

cursor_v2_coverage.browser_ga: Comprehensive - enterprise support, embedding, DOM tools
